articles.title: India's boardrooms are making AI decisions. Not all leaders feel ready for them.
articles.description: As AI's influence grows, leaders face the challenge of integrating it across operations and strategy. UC Berkeley Executive Education's 3-month online program equips mid-level and senior professionals with the skills to navigate AI and GenAI, fostering informed decision-making and driving organizational growth without requiring a technical background.

articles.title: Microsoft is retiring Teams’ Together Mode in favour of ...
articles.description: Microsoft is retiring its Together Mode feature in Teams on June 30, opting for a streamlined meeting experience centered on the Gallery view. This consolidation aims to simplify interfaces, reduce complexity, and enhance video quality and performance for all users. The move promises fewer clicks and smoother video, especially on less powerful devices, allowing for faster innovation in core features.

articles.title: Carpooling to using Metro, virtual meetings: Delhi Police joins austerity drive
articles.description: Delhi Police Commissioner Satish Golcha issued directive for officers, calling for immediate implementation of practical fuel-saving measures.

articles.title: Salesforce rewrites roles amid AI shift
articles.description: Salesforce is undergoing an AI-led transformation, redesigning jobs and reskilling its entire workforce to integrate AI agents into daily operations. Traditional roles are evolving into outcome-based positions, with AI handling repetitive tasks and employees focusing on higher-value work. The company emphasizes AI fluency, making it a core requirement for both existing employees and new hires.

articles.title: The Unfiltered Snabbit Story | The Startup Solving India’s Unorganised House Help Crisis
articles.description: AI may be transforming the world — but some of the biggest everyday problems still need human solutions. In this episode of The Business Today Podcast, Sakshi Batra sits down with Aayush Agarwal, Founder & CEO of Snabbit, the hyperlocal home services startup that scaled to over 50,000 jobs a day in less than a year. At a time when startups are racing toward AI-first models, Snabbit is building something very different: a human-first company solving one of urban India’s oldest and most frustrating problems — reliable house help. From managing 15,000+ service professionals across 140+ micro-markets to building trust, consistency, and operational discipline at scale, Aayush shares the unfiltered reality of building a category-defining startup in one of India’s toughest markets.

articles.title: Kolkata sees spurt in EV sales: Beltala PVD leads race with green vehicles outpacing diesel units
articles.description: Electric vehicles are now outnumbering diesel vehicles in Kolkata, with registration data from the Public Vehicles Department in Beltala showing a significant decline in diesel unit enrollments. This trend aligns with the central government's clean energy initiatives and a call to reduce fossil fuel consumption.

articles.title: Jim Bianco, head of one of American research company, agrees that AI panic is everywhere, but what AI doom-and-gloom story is missing
articles.description: Jim Bianco, a macro strategist, urges optimism regarding AI, arguing that fears of widespread job losses are misplaced. He explains AI's potential to streamline complex software stacks, freeing employees for higher-value tasks like judgment and strategy. Citing economic history, Bianco suggests AI could boost demand for skilled workers, similar to the internet's impact post-dot-com bubble.

articles.title: Digi-tech, health and space in focus for PM Modi’s visit to Norway

articles.title: All Biomods in Subnautica 2 and how to get them
articles.description: Subnautica 2 introduces Biomods, genetic modifications unlocked by scanning alien creatures, offering unique Active and Passive abilities. These range from enhanced movement and resource detection to predator distraction and improved survival stats like water and food efficiency. Players can also gain defensive and utility benefits such as threat alerts and bioluminescence.

articles.title: Uber CEO Dara Khosrowshahi on AI, EVs & Why India Could Become Uber’s Biggest Market
articles.description: Uber CEO Dara Khosrowshahi joins ETtech.com editor Samidha Sharma and Pranav Mukul for a wide-ranging conversation on India’s exploding mobility market, AI-powered engineering, EV adoption, bike taxis, autonomous vehicles, and why Uber believes India could eventually become its largest global market. From partnerships with the Adani Group to the future of food delivery, logistics, robotaxis, and AI agents booking rides. Dara explains how Uber is repositioning itself as a next-generation mobility and logistics platform. The conversation also dives into India’s startup ecosystem, public transport integration, social security for gig workers, and whether AI will augment or replace jobs.

articles.title: Delhi Confidential: Username Search

articles.description: Salesforce is significantly boosting AI spending, projecting hundreds of millions on Anthropic tokens, while halting software engineer hiring. CEO Marc Benioff highlights AI's role in enhancing engineer productivity by over 30%, enabling faster output and lower development costs. This strategic shift reflects a broader tech trend of leveraging AI for efficiency, with humans still integral to operations.

articles.title: WhatsApp is now testing the View-Once disappearing messages feature on iOS
articles.description: WhatsApp has been testing the View-Once disappearing messages feature on its Android app since last month, and we now hear it is being tested on iOS as well. This revelation comes from the folks at WABetaInfo, who spotted this View-Once disappearing messages feature on beta version 26.19.10.72 of WhatsApp for iOS. This feature can be enabled via the "After reading" option in the Default message timer > Message timer menu. On iOS, it allows the sender to set a countdown timer for 5 minutes, 1 hour, or 2 hours, after which the message(s) they sent will disappear automatically if read by...

articles.title: Auto MSMEs seek relief from carmakers
articles.description: New Delhi: Small and medium enterprises supplying auto components have urged large automobile manufacturers to share the burden of rising wages, energy prices and raw material costs, saying that many of them are under severe financial stress. They have also made a pitch for long-term vendor sustainability frameworks instead of purely transactional procurement practices.Industry body Federation of Indian Micro Small and Medium Enterprises (FISME) has approached the Society of Indian Auto Manufacturers that represents automakers, asking it to urge its members to speed up price revisions as well as make faster payments and provide support to suppliers through financing platforms such as the Trade Receivables Discounting System.Also read: In fast lane: The twin drivers behind MoTown India's fast & furious April showSMEs are struggling with sharp increases in labour and input costs, with wages rising by up to 35% in some regions and overall operating costs increasing more than 35% since March, the FISME said. The federation warned that small suppliers, already operating on thin margins and facing competition from countries like China and Vietnam, are finding it difficult to survive under existing annual pricing contracts.The industry body has also called for regular dialogue between large automakers and SME suppliers, saying that the long-term strength of India's automobile industry depends on the sustainability of thousands of small component manufacturers.In a letter to SIAM president Shailesh Chandra, FISME secretary-general Anil Bhardwaj said MSME component manufacturers are facing intense competition from suppliers in countries such as China, Thailand, Vietnam, Malaysia and Taiwan. ET has seen a copy of the letter, dated May 14.Unlike vehicle manufacturers operating in a relatively protected tariff environment, MSMEs have limited pricing power, extremely thin margins and weaker bargaining positions within supply chains.Also read: Passenger vehicle dispatches rise 25.4 per cent YoY at 4,37,312 units in April: SIAMGeopolitical tensions in the Middle East have worsened the situation by driving up energy prices and disrupting logistics, Bhardwaj wrote in the letter. Rising household costs and shortages of cooking gas have also triggered labour unrest in several industrial clusters.According to the FISME, prolonged protests and near riot-like situations forced many units to agree to wage hikes ranging from around 20% in some states to as high as 35% in Haryana.Input costs, including consumable oils, tools, power and raw materials, have increased by more than 35% since March, the federation said."For MSMEs, this is not a routine cyclical challenge. It is an existential squeeze," the letter said, adding that many MSMEs remain tied to annual rate contracts and delayed price revision cycles, even as their costs continue to rise immediately and sharply.The resulting pressure is eroding working capital, increasing debt levels and threatening production continuity, the FISME warned. It stressed that the long-term resilience of India's automobile industry depends not only on large manufacturers, but also on the sustainability of thousands of tier-II and -III component suppliers operating across industrial clusters.

articles.description: You've probably seen an ad for the rock-tuned Heavys H1H on the internet, and true to their word, they are in fact tuned for guitar-heavy rock. Here's how they compare to Apple's AirPods Max . AirPods Max 2 [left], Heavys H1H [right] As a major mainstream tech company, Apple has an incentive to design to accommodate as many people as possible. Its products may not necessarily have everything a particular person wants in an item. But even so, it will still be close enough to be acceptable for the majority of customers. articles.title: Bill Gates Foundation fully exits Microsoft, sells final $3.2 billion stake
articles.description: After over 25 years, the Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation has divested its final 7.7 million Microsoft shares, valued at $3.2 billion. This marks the end of Microsoft stock's long-standing dominance in the foundation's portfolio as it transitions to disbursing its endowment by 2045.

It works on both iOS and Android and across Instagram, WhatsApp, Messenger and your phone’s message notifications to search for contacts and to send and reply to messages. Naturally, you do have to be wearing the Neural Band accessory that comes bundled...
